texto: There is nothing so moving - not even acts of love or hate - as the discovery that one is not alone.

texto: The city is a cultural invention enforcing on the citizen knowledge of his own nature. And this we do not like. That we are aggressive beings easily given to violence that we get along together because we must more than because we want to and that the brotherhood of man is about as far from reality today as it was two thousand years ago that reason's realm is small that we never have been and never shall be created equal that if the human being is perfectible he has so far exhibited few symptoms - all are considerations of man from which space tends to protect us.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Robert Ardrey: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “The city is a cultural invention enforcing on the citizen knowledge of his own nature. And this w…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, city, country pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e country.

texto: Art is an adventure. When it ceases to be an adventure, it ceases to be art. Not all of us pursue the inaccessible landscapes of the twelve-tone scale, just as not all of us strive for inaccessible mountain-tops, or glory in storms at sea. But the human incidence is there. Could it be that these two impractical pursuits — of beauty and of adventure’s embrace — are simply two differing profiles of the same uniquely human reality?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Robert Ardrey que provoca temas ligados a motivacao. Ao questionar “Art is an adventure. When it ceases to be an adventure, it ceases to be art. texto: What we call patriotism, in other words, is a calculable force which, released by a predictable situation, will animate man in a manner no different from other territorial species.

texto: But we were born of risen apes, not fallen angels, and the apes were armed killers besides. And so what shall we wonder at? Our murders and massacres and missiles, and our irreconcilable regiments? Or our treaties whatever they may be worth; our symphonies however seldom they may be played; our peaceful acres, however frequently they may be converted into battlefields; our dreams however rarely they may be accomplished. The miracle of man is not how far he has sunk but how magnificently he has risen. We are known among the stars by our poems, not our corpses.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Robert Ardrey que provoca temas ligados a motivacao. Ao questionar “But we were born of risen apes, not fallen angels, and the apes were armed killers besides. And s…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre motivacao, esperanca, humanity, com foco em humanity.

texto: There is a virtue, I must presume, in shamelessness, since by placing on parade the things one does not know, one discovers that no one else knows either.
